rows.notes: Page title at top center. Handwriting is decorative and some letters (especially second word) are uncertain; read as 'Recepta Anni Praefnty' based on letter shapes and context as the page heading.

Small marginal entry at top-left showing 'tu' (abbreviation) with amount '60 0 0'. Location in left margin makes its purpose ambiguous (likely a total). Ink slightly faded and the abbreviation is unclear — interpreted as 'tu'. Amount column alignment clear.
Clear place name 'Gunner.' with no amounts written in the three-money columns. Text is legible and column lines are unambiguous.
Reads as 'Longwitham.' (handwriting clear). No amounts in the right-hand £/s/d columns.
Lettering is faint/ambiguous. I read the place name as 'Munkinroth.' (could alternatively be 'Munkinroth', 'Munkinroth' variants). No amounts visible. Chose a mid confidence due to uncertain letters.
Handwritten place name looks like 'South-nownington' (letters after 'South' are uncertain). No amounts present in the money columns.

Description reads 'Codlington Rey Ann and mich' (the 'Rey'/'Rey' reading is clear). Amount columns clearly show '20 00 0' aligned with the row (interpreted as £20 0s 0d). Column alignment unambiguous.
Reads as 'Yarmington. Ann 5' (some small flourish by the '5'). Amount reads '5 00 0' in the £/s/d columns; alignment is clear but the textual numeral next to the name is slightly stylized.
Clear place name 'Morton.' with no amounts in the money columns.

Description includes 'Chace-hill Ann.' and the right-hand money columns show three aligned numerals read as '3 01 9' — interpreted as £3 1s 9d. The '1' in the shillings column is slightly narrow but column alignment supports this reading.
Place name 'Thrup.' is legible; no monetary figures appear for this row.
Clear text 'Codlington Vicar.' Amount columns show '4 0 0' aligned with the row — read as £4 0s 0d. Vertical guide lines make alignment unambiguous.
Description 'Lucas Easton by Mr Broadwater' (Mr is abbreviated and handwriting is clear). Amount '2 0 0' is aligned and legible: £2 0s 0d.
Text reads 'Per Quitrents pd Ladyday 1713' (period/date clear). Right-hand columns show '5 7 2' — interpreted as £5 7s 2d. Alignment of digits to the three money columns confirms the parsing.
Short place name 'Warner.' clearly written; no amounts present.
Reads 'Ralph Marshall. capon' (the word 'capon' is legible). No monetary figures in the columns.
Single word 'Lord' visible but context and any trailing text are faint. No amounts visible. Lower confidence due to faintness and uncertainty if additional text was intended.
Reads 'Calcott.' though a small superscript mark/number appears above the line; no amounts in the £/s/d columns. Slight uncertainty about the superscript.
Text reads 'Edlington. Mills. Ann.' and the money columns show '4 10 10' aligned with the row. Pence 10 (<12) validates the column split and reading as £4 10s 10d; characters are fairly clear.
Place name 'Bampton.' clearly written; no amounts.
Place name 'Mountacute.' legible; no monetary entries.

Reads 'Hughs. Ann. and mie. 1713' (date and abbreviation visible). Money columns show '0 8 0' — interpreted as £0 8s 0d. Column alignment confirms the zero pounds in the leftmost of the three money columns.
Place name is legible as 'Greenaway.' No amounts present.
Clear 'Walker.' with no monetary figures.
Clear 'Franklyn.' and no amounts visible.
Short name 'Minne.' readable though faint. No amounts in columns for this line.
Reads 'Hart hall mich. 1713' (abbrev. 'mich' for Michaelmas). Money columns show '1 13 4' aligned with row. All numeric values fall into valid ranges; there is slight ink fade but digits are legible.
Description 'Leaden-hall.' is clear. Amounts read '0 10 0' — pounds column empty/zero, shillings 10, pence 0. Alignment with vertical guides is clear.

Text reads 'Sadler. Annum.' followed by small numerals in the manuscript which are somewhat smudged. Right-hand money columns show digits read as '2 5 10' — pence '10' is clear; the shillings digit is lighter and could be 5 or similar; chosen 5 based on alignment of the middle digit with shillings column.
Reads 'Molineux Annum.' and right columns clearly show '2 8 10' — interpreted as £2 8s 10d. Numerals align clearly in the three money columns.

Name line contains several items/notes: reads roughly 'Timlinkull Napper Ann. 1.18.8 Hopkins'. The money columns read '1 18 8' aligned with this row. The name spelling is uncertain (several letters are cramped); numeric alignment supports the monetary read.
Reads 'Clifton Henry. Ann.' and right-hand columns show '2 2 8' — interpreted as £2 2s 8d. The script for the names is slightly compressed but numbers are clearly aligned.
Place name 'Benjington.' legible though faint; no monetary figures present on the line.
Reads 'Broshouse.' (spelling follows manuscript). No amounts in the money columns.
Final line reads 'Southnewilton. Tenant' (tenant noted). No monetary figures visible. Name and 'Tenant' word are legible though the tail of the line is slightly faded.
